skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos, animate images, create talking avatars, add foley audio, and upscale video through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.summary: Create AI video workflows with inference.sh video models and utilities.
skill.author: toolshell

security_audit.summary: The scanner found many external command and URL patterns, which are expected because the skill is a wrapper for the networked inference.sh CLI. I found no evidence of malware, prompt injection, credential theft, or hidden code execution, but publication should include a warning that prompts and media URLs are sent to external services.

medium_findings.title: Networked CLI Execution Through infsh
medium_findings.description: The skill allows Bash(infsh *) and instructs users to run infsh login and infsh app run commands. This is legitimate for an inference.sh video skill, but it executes an external CLI that can contact remote services and process user prompts or media references.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool declaration and examples directly show intended execution of the infsh CLI. The context supports legitimate use, but remote execution through a service CLI is still a meaningful operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Remote Media References Sent To Generation Services
medium_findings.description: Several examples pass image_url, audio_url, video_url, or video lists to inference.sh apps. This is expected functionality, but users may expose private media URLs or sensitive prompt context to third-party generation providers.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples plainly send remote media references as model inputs. I did not find hidden exfiltration, so the concern is privacy exposure through normal skill operation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Documentation And Asset URLs
low_findings.description: The skill links to inference.sh documentation and displays a remote image. These URLs are user-visible and support documentation, but they confirm that the skill depends on external web resources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible Markdown links and image references. They do not appear to transmit secrets or run code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak cryptography matches are false positives from descriptive text and table entries, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, custom encryption, or cryptographic API use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain skill metadata and a utilities table header. Semantic review found no cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm selection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Broad infsh Bash Allowlist
dangerous_patterns.description: The allowed tool declaration permits any infsh subcommand. This is necessary for flexible app execution, but users should verify commands before allowing account, upload, or generation actions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowlist is explicit and broad within the infsh command namespace. It is constrained to one CLI, so the risk is lower than arbitrary shell execution.

content.value_statement: AI video creation often requires choosing the right model and command format. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured guidance for inference.sh video workflows.
actual_capabilities: Lists inference.sh video models for text-to-video, image-to-video, avatar, lipsync, and utility workflows.
Provides ready command patterns for running infsh video apps.
Shows examples for Veo, Grok Imagine Video, Wan, OmniHuman, Fabric, and PixVerse models.
Covers video utilities such as foley sound generation, upscaling, and media merging.
Points users to related inference.sh skills and documentation.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account.
Does not generate video locally or include model weights.
May send prompts and media URLs to external providers through inference.sh.
Model availability, pricing, and output quality depend on inference.sh services.

use_cases.title: Produce Social Clips
use_cases.description: Create short text-to-video clips for announcements, campaigns, or visual experiments.

use_cases.title: Animate Product Images
use_cases.description: Turn product or concept images into motion studies with image-to-video models.

use_cases.title: Build Avatar Narration
use_cases.description: Create talking-head or lipsync videos from a portrait and narration audio.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Video Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose an inference.sh model for a five-second text-to-video clip. I need a realistic style and optional audio.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Text-to-Video Command
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare an infsh command for a text-to-video prompt about a city sunrise. Include the model choice and explain the inputs.

prompt_templates.title: Animate an Existing Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image-to-video workflow using an existing image URL. Recommend a model and describe privacy checks before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multi-Step Video Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an inference.sh workflow that generates a video, adds foley audio, upscales it, and merges it with a second clip.

output_examples.input: I need a short cinematic video from a product prompt.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a suitable text-to-video model, drafts the infsh command, and explains expected inputs before execution.

output_examples.input: I have a portrait image and an audio narration.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects an avatar or lipsync model, describes required media URLs, and warns about sharing private media.

output_examples.input: I want to improve a generated silent clip.
output_examples.output: The assistant proposes foley generation, upscaling, and media merging steps using inference.sh utility apps.

best_practices: Review prompts and media URLs for private or sensitive content before running infsh commands.
Start with fast or lower-cost models before using higher-quality generation settings.
Save model names, prompts, and command settings so successful outputs can be reproduced.
anti_patterns: Do not paste confidential customer footage or private audio into public URLs.
Do not run generated infsh commands without checking the target app and inputs.
Do not assume every listed model supports the same duration, resolution, or audio options.

faq.question: Does this skill generate videos by itself?
faq.answer: No. It guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to use the inference.sh CLI for video generation.

faq.question: What tools are required?
faq.answer: You need the inference.sh CLI, shown as infsh, and an authenticated inference.sh account.

faq.question: Can it create videos from images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes image-to-video examples for models such as Wan and Seedance.

faq.question: Can it make talking avatar videos?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for OmniHuman, Fabric, and PixVerse lipsync workflows.

faq.question: Are private files safe to use?
faq.answer: Use caution. Media URLs and prompts may be sent to inference.sh and related model providers.

faq.question: Can it upscale or add audio to video?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill lists utility apps for foley sound, video upscaling, and media merging.
